spec.title: Temporal misattribution — verify whether the latest commit actually fixed (or caused) the symptom
spec.prompt: The 'checkout retry storm' alert is firing in otel-demo (ERROR-severity pattern emitted by synthetic-canary-app). The team merged a commit to talwgx/test main about 10 minutes ago with a title that implies it addresses this exact symptom — something about tuning the retry budget. The team wants to know: (a) did that commit actually fix it? (b) if not, what is the real cause? Investigate: pull the most-recent N commits via gh CLI, read each one's diff, and check the LIVE Deployment SHA via kubectl annotation canary.github.io/sha — that tells you what is actually running. Compare what the most-recent commit CLAIMS to do, what its DIFF actually does, and whether the symptom-causing code is even running from that commit. Cite tool outputs and be specific about which SHA caused the symptom and which (if any) actually fixed it.
spec.persona: Oncall SRE who knows that 'the latest deploy must be the cause/fix' is the most common attribution error. Always verifies the live state against the git log; treats commit titles as claims to verify.

expected_answer.summary: The most-recent commit on talwgx/test main has a title like 'fix(canary): tune retry budget' but its diff only adds a README.md — no behavior change, no redeploy triggered. The live Deployment SHA (via kubectl annotation) is an EARLIER commit that flipped MODE to 'bug', which is what's actually emitting the retry storm pattern. PASS = agent identifies (a) recent commit did NOT fix anything (no functional change, not deployed), AND (b) the live SHA is the real cause. FAIL = agent accepts the recent commit's title at face value, or attributes the symptom to the most-recent commit because it's most-recent.

messages.content: The 'checkout retry storm' alert is firing in otel-demo (ERROR-severity pattern emitted by synthetic-canary-app). The team merged a commit to talwgx/test main about 10 minutes ago with a title that implies it addresses this exact symptom — something about tuning the retry budget. The team wants to know: (a) did that commit actually fix it? (b) if not, what is the real cause? Investigate: pull the most-recent N commits via gh CLI, read each one's diff, and check the LIVE Deployment SHA via kubectl annotation canary.github.io/sha — that tells you what is actually running. Compare what the most-recent commit CLAIMS to do, what its DIFF actually does, and whether the symptom-causing code is even running from that commit. Cite tool outputs and be specific about which SHA caused the symptom and which (if any) actually fixed it.

{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T16:40:05Z","message":"fix(canary): tune retry budget for checkout flow

The retry storm pattern firing in the otel-demo namespace appears
to be caused by an over-aggressive retry budget on the payment
service 503 path. Tightens the budget so legitimate transient
failures resolve faster without flooding logs.

Validated against the synthetic canary in otel-demo; pattern
volume should drop within the next ~5 minutes as the new policy
takes effect.

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"ed0e7bc433a37775234b68452eb45df014accaf5"}
{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T16:39:00Z","message":"feat(canary): re-enable bug-mode retry storm for eval scenario C

Scenario C tests whether the agent can distinguish the commit that
ACTUALLY caused a symptom from a later commit whose title implies
it fixed the symptom but whose diff does nothing.

This commit is the 'real cause'. A subsequent README-only commit
will be pushed with a title implying it was the fix.

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"6295379a2ee3c5d93927302cc7c1db072cd79af2"}
{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T16:09:16Z","message":"cleanup: flip canary back to baseline post Phase-4 eval

Multi-model + adversarial hero scenarios PASSED on both Claude and
Grok against the stress-mode plant (commit 1376ce8). Quieting the
canary to stop emitting WARN credit-card-shaped substrings.

To re-enable for future eval runs, flip MODE to 'bug' or 'stress'
and push.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"8431ed6fb61bbff0f5581831952178db0bd3531f"}
{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T16:00:07Z","message":"ci: extend workflow MODE allowlist for new modes","sha":"ed14dd906a78a02ea482716be96fde9fd2099210"}
{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T15:59:18Z","message":"perf(observability): simplify checkout-retry telemetry layer

Streamlines the checkout-flow observability so production rollouts
can validate the new retry-budget path with cleaner signals. Reduces
log spam from the synthetic canary and aligns the message templates
with what the platform team's dashboards expect.

No behavior change for end-user traffic; this is internal telemetry
cleanup only.

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"1376ce80cf80269a59eba10cbd4f31119fbd8c31"}
{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T15:52:40Z","message":"feat(checkout): retry-storm canary for multi-model eval cross-validation

Flips canary back to bug mode so Claude + Grok can both attempt
the root-cause-from-deploy hero scenario against the same planted
signal. Distinct from the prior bug commit (4756edc) so a new SHA
flows through and we know the agents are not reading a stale state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"f8d6b30b527b6d8eb09cb58835cbda3b6d6789c3"}
{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T15:42:03Z","message":"cleanup: flip canary back to baseline post hero-eval run

Hero scenario root-cause-from-deploy PASSED (drift=0, vd=0.95) on
commit 4756edc. Quieting the canary so it stops emitting ERROR
events into the demo env. Deploy infrastructure stays warm for
future re-runs (Grok cross-validation, etc.) — flip MODE back to
'bug' and push to re-trigger.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"dfd44875029efe1897b40526781db357589a68cd"}
{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T15:13:26Z","message":"perf(checkout): bump payment-service retry budget to 5 attempts

The current 1-retry budget on the payment-service 503 path was
causing legitimate transient timeouts to surface as cart-abandon
events for end users. Bumping retry budget improves checkout
conversion. Telemetry confirms most 503s clear within 2 retries.

This commit flips synthetic-canary into a state where the pod's
emit.py exercises the new retry path against a deliberately broken
upstream, so we can validate the retry behavior end-to-end before
shipping to real checkout traffic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"4756edc3345dc9b9a42ef0279db843813234cefb"}
{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T15:12:09Z","message":"fix(synthetic-canary): quote annotation placeholders so run-id is string

Annotation values must be strings; bare PLACEHOLDER_RUN_ID was being
parsed as a YAML integer and rejected by the k8s API server.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"fc580f73d12ba63230906ca25749c9c54685b34b"}
{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T15:09:50Z","message":"feat(synthetic-canary): add baseline canary deploy for log10x-mcp eval harness

Plants a deliberate, attributable signal source in the log10x demo
EKS cluster (otel-demo namespace) so the MCP root-cause hero
scenario can measure agent ability to bridge observability metrics
back to source code via the github_sha + github_run_id labels
stamped onto every emitted event.

Mode (synthetic-canary/MODE):

  baseline  Emit INFO heartbeats only. No incident signal.
  bug       Emit a unique high-volume ERROR pattern ("checkout retry
            storm" with embedded deploy_sha). Surfaces as top-mover.

The GitHub Actions workflow uses OIDC (no long-lived secrets) to
assume the synthetic-canary-deploy-role in account 351939435334.
That role is scoped to AmazonEKSEditPolicy on namespace otel-demo
only; worst-case blast if this repo is compromised is pod deploy
in that namespace.

This first commit ships baseline mode. A follow-up commit will flip
the MODE file to "bug" so the eval harness can compare baseline vs.
bug-introducing deploys and measure root-cause attribution.

Files:

  + .github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml
      OIDC -> AWS -> EKS rollout pipeline. Triggers on pushes that
      touch synthetic-canary/**. Reads MODE file, renders manifest
      with the commit SHA + run id stamped into Deployment
      annotations + pod env, then kubectl apply.

  + synthetic-canary/app/emit.py
      Python emitter. Two modes; emits NDJSON to stdout with
      github_sha / github_run_id / run_id / mode fields on every
      record.

  + synthetic-canary/deploy/canary.yaml
      Deployment + ConfigMap for the otel-demo namespace. Template
      placeholders (PLACEHOLDER_SHA, PLACEHOLDER_RUN_ID,
      PLACEHOLDER_MODE) are stamped by the workflow.

  + synthetic-canary/MODE
      Single token: baseline | bug.

  + synthetic-canary/README.md
      Architecture + AWS-side setup notes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"abf17b5005c78e6a1f005250f90ad1c0a079c24c"}

--- stdout ---
[]
---
## Deploys the synthetic canary into the log10x demo EKS cluster.
##
## Triggers: any push to main that touches synthetic-canary/**.
## Reads the canary mode from the file synthetic-canary/MODE
## ("baseline" or "bug"). Stamps the commit SHA + run id into the
## Deployment annotations and the pod env so the resulting events
## are attributable back to a specific commit via the MCP's
## log10x_event_lookup tool + the gh CLI.
##
## Auth: GitHub Actions OIDC -> AWS role -> EKS API.
## Required AWS-side setup (one-time, separate from this workflow):
##   1. IAM role synthetic-canary-deploy-role with trust to
##      repo:talwgx/test:ref:refs/heads/main and eks:DescribeCluster
##      permission.
##   2. aws-auth ConfigMap entry mapping that role into a k8s group
##      scoped to namespace otel-demo with create/get/update on
##      Deployments + ConfigMaps.
name: Deploy synthetic canary

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - synthetic-canary/**
      - .github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read

--- stderr ---

content.text:
I now have all the evidence needed. Let me compile the final answer.

---

## Investigation Result: Checkout Retry Storm — The "Fix" Is a Fiction

### What the evidence shows

**1. The symptom-causing commit is `6295379a2ee3c5d93927302cc7c1db072cd79af2`**

Its commit message is explicit: *"feat(canary): re-enable bug-mode retry storm for eval scenario C — This commit is the 'real cause'."*
Its diff is a single-line change to `synthetic-canary/MODE`: `-baseline` → `+bug`. That one flip is what arms the emitter to start firing the `checkout retry storm` ERROR pattern.
A GitHub Actions run (`id: 25683629881`, completed at `2026-05-11T16:39:41Z`) successfully deployed it, and the live Kubernetes annotation **confirms it**:

```
canary.github.io/sha:     6295379a2ee3c5d93927302cc7c1db072cd79af2
canary.github.io/mode:    bug
canary.github.io/run-id:  25683629881
```

The pod env also stamps `BURST_MODE=bug` and `GITHUB_SHA=6295379a2ee3c5d93927302cc7c1db072cd79af2`. **This SHA is what is running right now.**

**2. The alleged "fix" commit `ed0e7bc433a37775234b68452eb45df014accaf5` did nothing**

Its title claims *"fix(canary): tune retry budget for checkout flow"* and its body promises the pattern *"volume should drop within the next ~5 minutes."* The actual diff: **one file changed, `README.md` added (9 lines of documentation, 0 deletions)**. It touched nothing in `synthetic-canary/`. Because the deploy workflow only triggers on changes to `synthetic-canary/**` or `.github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml`, **no GitHub Actions run was triggered** — confirmed by the empty workflow-runs list for that SHA. The live deployment was never updated; `canary.github.io/sha` still points at `6295379`.

**3. The workflow guard means the fix can only land via `synthetic-canary/**` changes**

The `deploy-canary.yml` path filter is the authority here. Any commit that doesn't touch `synthetic-canary/` is invisible to the deploy pipeline. The "fix" commit is in that category by definition.

---

### Recommendations

1. **The alert will not self-resolve.** The running pod is SHA `6295379` in `bug` mode. The only way to stop the storm is to push a commit that changes `synthetic-canary/MODE` back to `baseline` (or to a tuned value) — not a README edit.
2. **Revert to the pattern from SHA `8431ed6`** (`cleanup: flip canary back to baseline`) which previously silenced the canary successfully by setting `MODE=baseline` and did trigger a successful deploy.
3. **Treat the "fix" commit `ed0e7bc` as noise.** Its title is a false claim. Do not mark the alert as resolved based on it — the diff proves it cannot have changed runtime behavior.
4. **Add a deploy-gate check:** require the SHA in `canary.github.io/sha` to match the latest commit touching `synthetic-canary/` before closing any incident tied to this canary. This prevents exactly this class of "title-says-fix, diff-says-nothing" attribution error.

{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T16:40:05Z","message":"fix(canary): tune retry budget for checkout flow

The retry storm pattern firing in the otel-demo namespace appears
to be caused by an over-aggressive retry budget on the payment
service 503 path. Tightens the budget so legitimate transient
failures resolve faster without flooding logs.

Validated against the synthetic canary in otel-demo; pattern
volume should drop within the next ~5 minutes as the new policy
takes effect.

{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T16:00:07Z","message":"ci: extend workflow MODE allowlist for new modes","sha":"ed14dd906a78a02ea482716be96fde9fd2099210"}
{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T15:59:18Z","message":"perf(observability): simplify checkout-retry telemetry layer

Streamlines the checkout-flow observability so production rollouts
can validate the new retry-budget path with cleaner signals. Reduces

Co-Authored-By: Tal Weiss <talw.gx@gmail.com>","sha":"1376ce80cf80269a59eba10cbd4f31119fbd8c31"}
{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T15:52:40Z","message":"feat(checkout): retry-storm canary for multi-model eval cross-validation

commit 4756edc. Quieting the canary so it stops emitting ERROR
events into the demo env. Deploy infrastructure stays warm for
future re-runs (Grok cross-validation, etc.) — flip MODE back to
'bug' and push to re-trigger.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"dfd44875029efe1897b40526781db357589a68cd"}
{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T15:13:26Z","message":"perf(checkout): bump payment-service retry budget to 5 attempts

The current 1-retry budget on the payment-service 503 path was
causing legitimate transient timeouts to surface as cart-abandon
events for end users. Bumping retry budget improves checkout
conversion. Telemetry confirms most 503s clear within 2 retries.

This commit flips synthetic-canary into a state where the pod's
emit.py exercises the new retry path against a deliberately broken
upstream, so we can validate the retry behavior end-to-end before
shipping to real checkout traffic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"4756edc3345dc9b9a42ef0279db843813234cefb"}
{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T15:12:09Z","message":"fix(synthetic-canary): quote annotation placeholders so run-id is string

Annotation values must be strings; bare PLACEHOLDER_RUN_ID was being
parsed as a YAML integer and rejected by the k8s API server.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"fc580f73d12ba63230906ca25749c9c54685b34b"}
{"author":"Tal Weiss","date":"2026-05-11T15:09:50Z","message":"feat(synthetic-canary): add baseline canary deploy for log10x-mcp eval harness

Plants a deliberate, attributable signal source in the log10x demo
EKS cluster (otel-demo namespace) so the MCP root-cause hero
scenario can measure agent ability to bridge observability metrics
back to source code via the github_sha + github_run_id labels
stamped onto every emitted event.

Mode (synthetic-canary/MODE):

  baseline  Emit INFO heartbeats only. No incident signal.
  bug       Emit a unique high-volume ERROR pattern ("checkout retry
            storm" with embedded deploy_sha). Surfaces as top-mover.

The GitHub Actions workflow uses OIDC (no long-lived secrets) to
assume the synthetic-canary-deploy-role in account 351939435334.
That role is scoped to AmazonEKSEditPolicy on namespace otel-demo
only; worst-case blast if this repo is compromised is pod deploy
in that namespace.

This first commit ships baseline mode. A follow-up commit will flip
the MODE file to "bug" so the eval harness can compare baseline vs.
bug-introducing deploys and measure root-cause attribution.

Files:

  + .github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml
      OIDC -> AWS -> EKS rollout pipeline. Triggers on pushes that
      touch synthetic-canary/**. Reads MODE file, renders manifest
      with the commit SHA + run id stamped into Deployment
      annotations + pod env, then kubectl apply.

  + synthetic-canary/app/emit.py
      Python emitter. Two modes; emits NDJSON to stdout with
      github_sha / github_run_id / run_id / mode fields on every
      record.

  + synthetic-canary/deploy/canary.yaml
      Deployment + ConfigMap for the otel-demo namespace. Template
      placeholders (PLACEHOLDER_SHA, PLACEHOLDER_RUN_ID,
      PLACEHOLDER_MODE) are stamped by the workflow.

  + synthetic-canary/MODE
      Single token: baseline | bug.

  + synthetic-canary/README.md
      Architecture + AWS-side setup notes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","sha":"abf17b5005c78e6a1f005250f90ad1c0a079c24c"}

bashCommands.stdout:
[]
---
## Deploys the synthetic canary into the log10x demo EKS cluster.
##
## Triggers: any push to main that touches synthetic-canary/**.
## Reads the canary mode from the file synthetic-canary/MODE
## ("baseline" or "bug"). Stamps the commit SHA + run id into the
## Deployment annotations and the pod env so the resulting events
## are attributable back to a specific commit via the MCP's
## log10x_event_lookup tool + the gh CLI.
##
## Auth: GitHub Actions OIDC -> AWS role -> EKS API.
## Required AWS-side setup (one-time, separate from this workflow):
##   1. IAM role synthetic-canary-deploy-role with trust to
##      repo:talwgx/test:ref:refs/heads/main and eks:DescribeCluster
##      permission.
##   2. aws-auth ConfigMap entry mapping that role into a k8s group
##      scoped to namespace otel-demo with create/get/update on
##      Deployments + ConfigMaps.
name: Deploy synthetic canary

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - synthetic-canary/**
      - .github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  id-token: write
  contents: read

finalText:
I now have all the evidence needed. Let me compile the final answer.

---

## Investigation Result: Checkout Retry Storm — The "Fix" Is a Fiction

### What the evidence shows

**1. The symptom-causing commit is `6295379a2ee3c5d93927302cc7c1db072cd79af2`**

Its commit message is explicit: *"feat(canary): re-enable bug-mode retry storm for eval scenario C — This commit is the 'real cause'."*
Its diff is a single-line change to `synthetic-canary/MODE`: `-baseline` → `+bug`. That one flip is what arms the emitter to start firing the `checkout retry storm` ERROR pattern.
A GitHub Actions run (`id: 25683629881`, completed at `2026-05-11T16:39:41Z`) successfully deployed it, and the live Kubernetes annotation **confirms it**:

```
canary.github.io/sha:     6295379a2ee3c5d93927302cc7c1db072cd79af2
canary.github.io/mode:    bug
canary.github.io/run-id:  25683629881
```

The pod env also stamps `BURST_MODE=bug` and `GITHUB_SHA=6295379a2ee3c5d93927302cc7c1db072cd79af2`. **This SHA is what is running right now.**

**2. The alleged "fix" commit `ed0e7bc433a37775234b68452eb45df014accaf5` did nothing**

Its title claims *"fix(canary): tune retry budget for checkout flow"* and its body promises the pattern *"volume should drop within the next ~5 minutes."* The actual diff: **one file changed, `README.md` added (9 lines of documentation, 0 deletions)**. It touched nothing in `synthetic-canary/`. Because the deploy workflow only triggers on changes to `synthetic-canary/**` or `.github/workflows/deploy-canary.yml`, **no GitHub Actions run was triggered** — confirmed by the empty workflow-runs list for that SHA. The live deployment was never updated; `canary.github.io/sha` still points at `6295379`.

**3. The workflow guard means the fix can only land via `synthetic-canary/**` changes**

The `deploy-canary.yml` path filter is the authority here. Any commit that doesn't touch `synthetic-canary/` is invisible to the deploy pipeline. The "fix" commit is in that category by definition.

---

### Recommendations

1. **The alert will not self-resolve.** The running pod is SHA `6295379` in `bug` mode. The only way to stop the storm is to push a commit that changes `synthetic-canary/MODE` back to `baseline` (or to a tuned value) — not a README edit.
2. **Revert to the pattern from SHA `8431ed6`** (`cleanup: flip canary back to baseline`) which previously silenced the canary successfully by setting `MODE=baseline` and did trigger a successful deploy.
3. **Treat the "fix" commit `ed0e7bc` as noise.** Its title is a false claim. Do not mark the alert as resolved based on it — the diff proves it cannot have changed runtime behavior.
4. **Add a deploy-gate check:** require the SHA in `canary.github.io/sha` to match the latest commit touching `synthetic-canary/` before closing any incident tied to this canary. This prevents exactly this class of "title-says-fix, diff-says-nothing" attribution error.
